hook.prompt: Before executing this bash command, verify it is safe. Check for: 1) No destructive operations (rm -rf, etc.) without explicit user confirmation, 2) No exposure of secrets or credentials, 3) No network requests to untrusted sources. If the command appears unsafe, suggest a safer alternative.
A file was just written. Briefly note: 1) What type of file was created, 2) Any potential issues to be aware of (missing imports, incomplete implementation, etc.).
Session started. Remind the user they can use /help to see available commands and plugins.
Before concluding, verify: 1) All requested tasks are complete, 2) Any created files are properly saved, 3) The user has been given clear next steps if applicable.
